rulings.Case Title: JOHN COWAN VS. DANIEL LOPEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-15-546196 WITH CASE CGC-13-531107
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 17, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF JOHN COWAN'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-15-546196 WITH CASE CGC-13-531107. Plaintiff John Cowan's Motion to Consolidate Case number 546196 with case number 531107 is GRANTED. Plaintiff John Cowan sufficiently establishes that Defendant's alleged defamation as to Plaintiff John Cowan and Plaintiff Timothy Broderick involve several common questions of law or fact. (Cal. Code Civ. Pro. §1048.) This Court has carefully considered the moving and opposition papers and has determined consolidation will avoid unnecessary costs or delay and will not prejudice Defendant. (Ibid.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: MARTHE SCHREIBER VS. LEE, STEPHEN K., D/B/A STEPHEN K. LEE ENTERPRISES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 17, 2015, Line 13, PLAINTIFF MARTHE SCHREIBER'S Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint. The motion is granted. Plaintiff has leave to file the amended complaint. The summary judgment set for 10/15/15 is off calendar. See State Compensation Ins. Fund v. Sup. Crt. (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1130-1131. Defendants may apply to department 206 for a trial continuance to insure that the motion for summary judgment can be re-calendared. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CAROL GILBERT, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO ELLIS O'FARRELL PARKING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 17, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF CAROL GILBERT, INC.'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint. Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint is denied. Courts liberally permit amendments to the complaint at any stage of the proceeding as long as granting leave does not result in prejudice to the opposition. Contrary to Plaintiff's position, Plaintiff learned of the factual basis of the proposed amendments three years ago. To grant the motion would cause undue delay and prejudice to Defendant as trial is set for September 28, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: ARIANA AGENBROAD VS. DIVYENDRA SINGH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answer To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And Fo Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, September 17, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT DIVYENDRA SINGH'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answer To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And Fo Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Dennis Hyde, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The unopposed motion is granted. Plaintiff Ariana Agenbroad will respond without objections to Defendant Divyendra Singh's Form Interrogatories-General; Special Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories to Plaintiff, Set 2; Demand for Inspection and Production of Documents and Things, Set Number One; and Request for Production of Documents, Set 2; and pay monetary sanctions in the amount of $208.00 to Defendant; within 15 days of service of the order. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org and hydelaw@pacbell.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm the court day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare an order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FLORIAN GOETTE VS. FERGUS MORONEY (CCP 170.6 CHALLENGE AGAINST JUDGE KAHN; PENDING CCP170.1 CHALLENGE TO JUDGE QUINN)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Be Allowed To Conduct Limited Discovery
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, September 17, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF FLORIAN GOETTE'S Notice Of Motion To Be Allowed To Conduct Limited Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Dennis Hyde, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org and hydelaw@pacbell.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm the court day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/JPT)
